topic: car making a noise — trying to diagnose

turns.content: bro my car is making this weird grinding noise when i brake. only on the left side

That sounds like it could be a brake issue — possibly worn brake pads on the left front. When the pad material wears down, the metal backing plate starts grinding against the rotor. How long has it been making the noise?
like 3 days maybe. it started quiet now its getting louder. and sometimes i feel a vibration in the steering wheel too

The vibration in the steering wheel along with the grinding strongly suggests it's the front left brake. The rotor might be getting scored or warped from the metal-on-metal contact. I'd recommend getting it looked at soon — driving on grinding brakes can damage the rotor to the point where it needs full replacement instead of just new pads.
man thats what i was afraid of. i cant afford a big repair right now. u think i can just do the pads myself? ive seen youtube videos on it

Brake pads are actually one of the more doable DIY jobs if you're handy. You'd need a jack, jack stands, a lug wrench, a C-clamp to push the caliper piston back, and the new pads. The whole job can take about an hour per side. But — if the rotor is already damaged from the grinding, you might need to replace or resurface that too, which is a bit more involved.
how do i know if the rotor is messed up

Once you pull the wheel off and remove the caliper, look at the rotor surface. If you see deep grooves, scoring marks, or if it looks uneven, it probably needs replacing. You can also run your finger across it — if it feels like a record with ridges instead of smooth, that's a bad sign. Also check the rotor thickness; there's usually a minimum spec stamped on it.
ok that makes sense. what about the caliper itself can that cause grinding too

Good question — yes, a sticking caliper can definitely cause grinding. If the caliper isn't releasing properly, it keeps the pad pressed against the rotor even when you're not braking. That causes constant friction, heat buildup, and grinding. You'd notice that side pulling when you brake, or the wheel being hot after driving. But worn pads are still the most common cause, so start there.
ok bet. ima try to do it this weekend. autozone got the pads for like 40 bucks. way better than paying a shop 300+

Definitely way cheaper doing it yourself. AutoZone is great — they'll even loan you tools for free if you need a caliper tool or anything. Just make sure you get pads that match your exact make and model. And use jack stands, not just the jack. Safety first.
wait one more thing bro. should i do both sides even if only the left is grinding or just the one side

Great question. It's generally recommended to do both sides at the same time — even if only one side is grinding, the other is probably close behind. Plus, having uneven pad wear on left and right can cause the car to pull to one side when braking. Since the pads aren't that expensive, doing both gives you peace of mind and even braking.
yeah for sure bro. cant be out here with no car thats my whole income right now. thanks for the help ima watch a couple more videos tonight and hit autozone tomorrow
Good plan. ChrisFix on YouTube has great brake pad tutorials if you need a recommendation. Take your time with it and you'll be fine. Let me know how it goes!
